summary: Fluoroquinolone class includes antibiotics targeting DNA functions by inhibiting critical enzymes involved in DNA replication; specifically, ciprofloxacin acts via inhibition of both DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV. The mechanism leads to DNA damage resulting from uncontrolled unwinding processes necessary during cellular division cycles. To directly address your query regarding an antibiotic affecting DNA functionality without additional context or specific alternatives provided here beyond 'fluoroquinolones,' we conclude it would be most accurate based upon available information if referring generally towards any member within said category where C represents one example fitting those criteria perfectly well according current medical literature findings presented above.
